created_by: Wilderculture WILD Toolbox session (Claude) — fresh v3.2 build; supersedes the v2.0 record. UKHab name corrected to 'Modified grassland'; g4 confirmed terminal at Level 3.

ukhab_identity.composite_pk_note: v3.2 composite PK is ukhab_level4 + wilderculture_subtype; where a UKHab code has a single functional type (as here) the subtype is null and the key degenerates to the code alone. NOTE: g4 'Modified grassland' is terminal at Level 3 in UKHab v2.01 — there is no Level 4 subdivision; surveys and the Biodiversity Metric use g4 directly as the mapped habitat, so it is recorded at ukhab_level4 as the operative code with level3 = level4 = g4.
ukhab_identity.secondary_codes_notes: None at type level. Per-parcel surveys may attach management or species-related secondary codes.

sources.title: UK Habitat Classification v2.01
sources.specific_claim: Grassland Level 3 codes are g1 Acid, g2 Calcareous, g3 Neutral, g4 Modified. g4 'Modified grassland' has no Level 4 subdivision and is used as the operative/mapped code. Coding alternates letter/number (g3b5), confirming a trailing-suffix Level 4 would be g4-letter, none of which exists.

sources.title: Windburn Wind Farm — UKHab/NVC Habitat Report (uses 'Modified Grassland (UKHab g4)')
sources.specific_claim: Applied survey confirms g4 used directly as the Modified Grassland mapped code with no Level 4 suffix.

nvc_communities: MG6 (Lolium perenne–Cynosurus cristatus) as the improved-pasture analogue; MG7 where rye-grass dominant; transitional toward MG5/MG9/MG10 in longer-established, less-improved stands
cross_references.nvc_communities_notes: Modified grassland is defined by agricultural modification rather than an NVC community; the analogues above span the modified↔semi-improved range. Recorded as guidance, not a parcel classification.
cross_references.phase1_equivalent: B4 Improved grassland / B2.2 Semi-improved neutral grassland (depending where on the continuum a parcel sits)
cross_references.bng_metric_note: Low distinctiveness in the Biodiversity Metric. Long-established, botanically recovering stands may merit reassessment toward 'Other neutral grassland' (g3c) on survey — a per-parcel judgement.
cross_references.wild_sheet_ref_note: Sheet-less production-spectrum grassland (no semi-natural field sheet).

sources.title: Handbook for Phase 1 Habitat Survey (B4 Improved / B2.2 Semi-improved grassland)
sources.specific_claim: Modified grassland maps to Phase 1 B4/B2.2 across its range. Standard reference, not independently re-verified this session.

ecology.description: A wide spectrum of improved and semi-improved permanent swards subject to agricultural modification — reseeding, fertilising, draining or heavy grazing — but which are not temporary leys. Spans recently modified, ryegrass-dominated production swards through to long-established permanent pasture with increasing botanical diversity. Ecological character is set by time since last cultivation and management intensity since.
condition_indicators_good: Perennial ryegrass and/or improved fescues present but with increasing botanical diversity over time
Dense basal ground cover with minimal bare ground
Stable, well-drained sward without rush invasion or poaching
Some neutral-grassland forbs returning in longer-established stands
condition_indicators_poor: Soft/sharp rush (Juncus effusus / J. acutiflorus) dominant — impeded drainage or compaction
Annual meadow-grass (Poa annua) dominant — overgrazing, compaction, poor management
Docks, thistles, ragwort establishing in open sward
Bare ground and poaching from over-grazing
ecosystem_functions: Forage production — primary function; lower peak DM than a fresh ley but consistent year-round production from permanent root systems
Soil carbon storage — permanent, uncultivated swards hold more soil organic carbon than rotational leys (a key permanent-pasture value)
Soil structure and stability — undisturbed permanent root systems resist erosion and compaction recovery
Increasing pollinator and invertebrate resource with time since modification
ecology.whole_system_role: The working permanent grassland of most farms: a production sward across a value continuum. Recently modified stands behave like a lower-yielding ley; long-established stands accrue soil-carbon and biodiversity value approaching semi-natural grassland. Its role — and its WILD zone — depend on where on that continuum a parcel sits.

successional_neighbours.note: A ley left down long enough, or a reseed not renewed, becomes modified permanent grassland.
Long-established, low-input modified grassland recovers botanically toward other neutral grassland (g3c).
Compaction or failed drainage drives rush invasion.

sources.title: UK National Ecosystem Assessment Technical Report — Chapter 6: Grasslands
sources.specific_claim: Permanent grassland holds greater soil carbon and lower digestibility than temporary grassland; improved grassland with fertiliser yields 10–12 t DM/ha. Carried from verified v2.0 sourcing; primary not re-fetched this session.

ukhab_condition.condition_grade_note: Assigned per parcel. Descriptors below define the production-criteria scale for modified grassland.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Excellent / optimal
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Dense, productive sward; good ground cover; no rush/weed dominance; well-drained.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Good
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Productive with minor weed-grass or localised rush/poaching.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Moderate
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Patchy productivity; rush or weed-grass spreading; drainage/compaction issues emerging.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Poor
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Rush- or weed-dominated; low productivity; significant bare ground/poaching.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Degraded / reclassify
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Sward function lost; reclassify (e.g. to rush pasture) or restore.

sources.title: Improving grassland productivity (UK average and potential yields)
sources.specific_claim: UK average grassland ~7 t DM/ha, capable of >12 t with management; sward condition (rush, weed grasses, ground cover) drives productivity grade.

abiotic_template.parent_material_geology: Variable — improved over a wide range of substrates
abiotic_template.aspect_exposure: Enclosed agricultural ground, generally sheltered; extends higher and wetter than leys
abiotic_template.bioclimate_notes: Drainage status is the key local variable — impeded drainage tips modified grassland toward rush pasture.

elemental_conditions.convention_note: REGENERATE TARGET STATE (default zone; Schema v3.2 convention): dials describe the productive target. NOTE the actual current state of a g4 parcel varies widely along the modified↔semi-natural continuum — for a parcel assigned Protect & Restore, describe the current state instead.

water.descriptor: Target: free-draining but moisture-retentive soil. Real parcels often sit lower (impeded drainage, rush risk).

earth.descriptor: Target: stable, aggregated permanent topsoil with accruing organic matter; permanent swards build soil carbon.

air.descriptor: Target: aerated rooting zone; compaction and waterlogging are the common departures.

fire.descriptor: Target: solid primary production from permanent root systems — consistent rather than peak.

successional_envelope.current_stage: Permanent modified sward; position on the modified↔semi-natural continuum varies by parcel
range_of_natural_variability: Mid-spectrum permanent pasture with returning forbs
Long-established, low-input permanent pasture recovering toward other neutral grassland (g3c)
successional_envelope.successional_pathway: Two-way: continued improvement holds it production-side; low input and time move it toward semi-natural neutral grassland; drainage failure moves it toward rush pasture.

wild_assessment.energy_flow_role: Consistent, moderate-to-high primary production with year-round throughput from permanent roots; soil-carbon accrual is a distinguishing energy/storage role versus a rotational ley.
wild_assessment.wild_zone_rationale: DEFAULT ONLY — flag for per-parcel confirmation. As a working production sward the typical assignment is Regenerate (lift soil function, structure and sward quality). BUT g4 is the most zone-variable habitat in the set: a recently reseeded parcel is firmly Regenerate, while a long-established, botanically recovering permanent pasture with soil-carbon value may belong in Protect & Restore. Assign per parcel against the successional position and ecological value.
wild_assessment.disturbance_regime: Supplied — grazing/cutting, fertilising, drainage; the manager holds the dial. In a Protect & Restore parcel the aim shifts to maintaining the recovering state.

wild_management_target.detail: Default (Regenerate): maintain/lift productive sward function — ground cover, drainage, fertility, controlling rush and weeds. For a Protect & Restore parcel: hold and extend the botanical recovery and soil-carbon store with low-intensity grazing. Re-grade trigger: two seasons measured a band above/below → re-grade.

wild_assessment.ecological_value_notes: REPRESENTATIVE/typical value only — genuinely VARIABLE per parcel (range 1–4). Recently modified g4: grade 1–2 (minimal value, ryegrass-dominated). Long-established permanent pasture within g4: grade 3–4 (botanical recovery, pollinator resource and soil-carbon value). Value is directly proportional to time since last cultivation. Assess per field; do not apply the headline grade 2 to a long-established stand.

rarity_irreplaceability.notes: Common and replaceable, though old permanent pasture's soil-carbon store is slow to rebuild once lost.

diversity.notes: Variable — low in recently modified swards (1), moderate (3) in long-established pasture.

energy_flow_contribution.notes: Steady primary production plus soil-carbon storage; the storage role rises with sward age.

wild_assessment.naturalistic_lu_ha_note: Not applicable under the Regenerate default. (If a parcel is assigned Bioregionally Wilder, populate.)
wild_assessment.mosaic_role_note: Not applicable under the Regenerate default.
wild_assessment.preferential_grazing_magnet_note: False under the default; productive g4 can be a grazing draw in a mixed holding but is not a wilder-mosaic patch.
wild_assessment.process_indicators_note: Empty under the Regenerate default (state target).

sources.title: WILD Method Statement — The Science Behind WILD
sources.specific_claim: Framework basis for the dials, the per-parcel zone judgement and the target-state convention. Overlay layer, not L1–L3 core data.

forage.years_unimproved: variable — 5 (recently modified) to 50+ (old permanent pasture); assess per field
forage.overall_palatability_notes: Good but below a fresh ley — higher proportion of native grasses and older sward composition. Palatability falls as rush/weed grasses increase.

forage_yield_by_grade.notes: GROSS production. Well-anchored: AHDB UK average ~7 t DM/ha, >12 t achievable with management; ScienceDirect 2018 (L1) benchmark 8.7 t/ha; Agrii reseed ~8.75 t vs tired ~7 t. Grade 1 = well-managed permanent sward (not a fresh ley). Apply utilisation_factor for utilisable/LU.
Typical/average permanent pasture (around the UK 7 t mean and below).
Tired pasture, weed-grass ingress; estimated from the decline trajectory.

forage_yield_literature.notes: GROSS DM/ha/yr. Among the best-sourced ranges in the toolbox: multiple independent L1–L2 sources converge (AHDB average 7 t; ScienceDirect benchmark 8.7 t; UK NEA improved-with-fertiliser 10–12 t). The wide spread reflects the g4 spectrum — recently modified (approaching ley performance) to old permanent pasture (approaching semi-natural baseline). The ~10 t commonly quoted for good permanent pasture is utilised; store gross and apply utilisation.

sources.title: Grassland futures in Great Britain — productivity assessment and scenarios
sources.specific_claim: UK benchmark permanent grassland productivity ~8.7 t DM/ha. Carried from verified v2.0 sourcing.

sources.title: Improving grassland productivity
sources.specific_claim: UK average ~7 t DM/ha; >12 t achievable with management.

sources.title: Reseeding returns (DM and ME of reseed vs tired pasture)
sources.specific_claim: Reseeded grassland ~8,750 kg DM/ha vs tired pasture ~7,000 kg; ME 11.5 vs 10.5 MJ/kg DM. Industry source.

utilisation_factor.notes: Permanent pasture is more often set-stocked than rotational, so realistic utilisation tends to sit below a managed ley (AHDB: set-stocked <50%, rotational 60–70%, paddock 75–80%). Default 0.70 assumes reasonable rotational management; lower for set-stocked. Ground-truth per farm.

sources.title: Utilisation is key to a successful grazing system
sources.specific_claim: Utilisation by grazing system: set-stocked <50%, rotational 60–70%, paddock 75–80%.

forage.conservation_lu_ha_note: Null under the Regenerate default (LU derived via utilisation). For a g4 parcel assigned Protect & Restore, a direct conservation_lu_ha should be populated instead.

nutritional_quality_by_grade.notes: Below a fresh ley due to native-grass proportion and older sward. Agrii: tired pasture ME ~10.5 vs reseed ~11.5. CP depends on clover content and N inputs.

nutritional_quality_sources.title: Reseeding returns (ME of reseed vs tired pasture)
nutritional_quality_sources.specific_claim: Old/tired grassland ME ~10.5 MJ/kg DM vs reseed ~11.5.

nutritional_quality_sources.title: UK NEA — Grasslands (digestibility)
nutritional_quality_sources.specific_claim: Digestibility lower in permanent than temporary grassland.

seasonal_forage_curve.spring: Moderate–high — permanent roots give reliable early growth
seasonal_forage_curve.summer: Moderate — growth steadier but lower-peak than a ley; dips in drought (shallower improved roots)
seasonal_forage_curve.winter: Low — minimal growth; permanent sward tolerates deferred/winter grazing better than a ley

optimal_grazing_animals.notes: Permanent pasture suits a wide range incl. hardier types; breed-level matching via the Animal entity.
Well-matched; mixed grazing helps sward and parasite management.

unsuitable_animals.reason: g4 is a forgiving working sward; matching is about efficiency and sward goals, not exclusion.

timing_windows.preferred_season: Year-round grazing potential; rotational April–October with deferred/winter grazing tolerated
timing_windows.rationale: Permanent root systems tolerate a wider grazing window than a ley
timing_windows.avoid: Winter poaching on wet ground (rush invasion risk); over-grazing that opens the sward to weeds
timing_windows.per_species_notes: Mixed cattle/sheep grazing aids sward structure and breaks parasite cycles.

management.intensity_guidance: Stocking is SCHEME-TIED and never averaged. Derive LU/ha from utilised DM ÷ 5.5 t per LU: at grade 1, gross 7–10 t × ~0.70 utilisation ≈ 4.9–7.0 t utilised ≈ ~0.9–1.3 LU/ha as a planning figure (lower than a ley). England: GLU (Natural England/ELM); Scotland: LU (SAC/FAS TN686). Take each from its own scheme — do not average. For a Protect & Restore parcel, use a conservation_lu_ha instead.
management.monitoring_protocol: Plate meter / sward stick; track rush and weed-grass cover, ground cover and drainage; watch for botanical recovery indicators in long-established stands (may trigger reassessment toward g3c or a zone change). Apply the re-grade trigger.
management.reseed_decision_note: The reseed-vs-retain choice is central to g4: reseeding lifts yield/ME but resets soil carbon and any botanical recovery. Weigh production gain against the soil-carbon and biodiversity value of an established sward — a Regenerate-vs-Protect decision in microcosm.

bng_unit_value.value_note: Low distinctiveness (Grassland — Modified grassland). Long-established, recovering stands may reassess higher on survey; the soil-carbon value of old permanent pasture is not captured by the metric.

sources.title: Statutory Biodiversity Metric
sources.specific_claim: Modified grassland (g4) is low distinctiveness. Exact current label to confirm against the live metric.

key_positive_indicators: Returning neutral-grassland forbs (e.g. Ranunculus acris, Plantago lanceolata, Centaurea nigra in older stands)
Diverse grass sward in long-established pasture
species_list.note: Skeleton only — full species records and the Habitat↔Species join built in Phase 2B. The positive-indicator set differs by zone: production indicators for Regenerate parcels, recovery indicators for Protect & Restore parcels.

fields_fully_populated: ukhab_identity — corrected this session: g4 confirmed terminal at Level 3, official name 'Modified grassland', single-type (subtype null)
forage yield (grades 1–2) and nutrition — well-sourced L1–L2 (UK NEA, ScienceDirect 2018, AHDB)
fields_partially_populated: wild_zone — Regenerate DEFAULT only; g4 is the most zone-variable habitat (per-parcel confirmation required)
ecological_value_grade — set to representative 2 but genuinely VARIABLE 1–4 along the modified↔semi-natural continuum
forage/nutrition grades 3–5 — estimated; cross_references NVC/Phase1 from standard refs (medium)
fields_pending_research: Per-parcel zone and ecological-value assignment (inherent to g4 — cannot be fixed at type level)
Re-fetch of UK NEA and ScienceDirect 2018 primaries (carried from verified v2.0 sourcing)
Soil-carbon figures for old vs recently modified permanent pasture (Phase 2B)
Per-farm ground-truthing of utilisation and derived LU/ha
data_completeness.notes: Forage evidence is among the strongest in the toolbox. The defining feature of g4 is its breadth: one UKHab code spanning a production-to-semi-natural continuum, so ecological value and WILD zone are explicitly per-parcel and flagged as such rather than fixed. Identity corrected (g4 terminal at L3; official name restored). Gross-vs-utilised separated. Completes the production trio (c1b ryegrass, c1b herbal, g4).

sources.title: UK Habitat Classification v2.01
sources.specific_claim: g4 Modified grassland; terminal at L3.

sources.title: UK National Ecosystem Assessment — Grasslands
sources.specific_claim: Permanent grassland carbon/digestibility; improved yield 10–12 t.

sources.title: Grassland futures in Great Britain
sources.specific_claim: Benchmark permanent grassland productivity ~8.7 t DM/ha.

sources.title: Improving grassland productivity
sources.specific_claim: UK average ~7 t DM/ha; >12 t achievable.
